title: Overactive Bladder Relief with 8 Effective Herbal Creams

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for overactive bladder because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help calm the muscles in the bladder and reduce symptoms of urgency and frequency.

Tannins are good for overactive bladder because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help relax the muscles in the bladder and reduce spasms, resulting in improved bladder control and reduced urgency.

Isorhapontigenin is good for overactive bladder because it has been shown to relax the bladder muscles and reduce the frequency of urination by blocking the action of certain chemicals that can cause bladder contractions.

Polyphenols may be good for overactive bladder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the bladder and reduce muscle spasms, leading to improved urinary control and reduced symptoms.

Ursolic acid is good for overactive bladder because it helps to relax the muscles in the bladder and reduce spasms, allowing for more controlled urination and relief from frequent trips to the bathroom.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as nettle, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with different health issues, including overactive bladder. The cream made from this plant is good for overactive bladder because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the bladder and urinary tract. This can help relax the muscles in the bladder and reduce the need to urinate frequently. Urtica dioica cream may also help reduce symptoms of urgency and frequency, which are common problems for people with overactive bladder. Additionally, the cream may have a soothing effect on the bladder and urinary tract, making it easier to urinate and reducing the risk of infections. Some people find that using Urtica dioica cream can help them manage their symptoms and improve their quality of life. Research has shown that Urtica dioica can help reduce the symptoms of overactive bladder in some people, but more studies are needed to confirm its effectiveness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Equisetum arvense herbal cream that helps with overactive bladder, start by gathering the right ingredients. You'll need dried Equisetum arvense herb, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a beeswax or candelilla wax to thicken the cream. First, infuse the dried herb in the carrier oil by mixing them together in a clean glass jar and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax or candelilla wax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short bursts. Once the wax is melted, slowly pour it into the infused oil mixture while whisking constantly to avoid lumps. Continue whisking until the mixture thickens and forms a smooth cream. Pour the cream into a clean container, let it cool and harden, and then use it as needed to help soothe and calm the bladder muscles.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for overactive bladder

Valerenol is good for overactive bladder because it has a relaxing effect on the muscles in the bladder, which helps to control sudden and frequent urination.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help with overactive bladder (OAB). The main ingredient, valerian root, has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system and relax muscles. When applied topically as a cream, it can help soothe the bladder muscles and reduce spasms that cause OAB symptoms. This can lead to fewer bathroom trips and less discomfort. Valerian root has anti-inflammatory properties, which can also help reduce irritation and inflammation in the bladder. Additionally, the cream can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for OAB. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms, Valeriana officinalis cream may provide relief for people experiencing OAB symptoms. It can be used in combination with other treatments, such as lifestyle changes and medications, to help manage symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Equisetum arvense herbal cream for overactive bladder, you'll need dried horsetail plant, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a natural emulsifier like beeswax. First, steep one tablespoon of dried horsetail in two tablespoons of hot water for 10-15 minutes to make a tea. Let it cool, then strain the liquid and discard the solids. Next, mix the cooled tea with one cup of carrier oil in a small saucepan and gently heat it on low heat until the mixture reaches 120-130°F (49-54°C). Remove the saucepan from the heat and add one tablespoon of beeswax to the mixture, stirring constantly until it's fully melted and combined. Pour the mixture into a clean glass container and let it cool and solidify completely. Once set, your Equisetum arvense herbal cream is ready to use. To apply, gently massage a small amount onto the lower abdomen area to help soothe and calm overactive bladder symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for overactive bladder

remedies.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna cream is a natural remedy that may help with overactive bladder. This cream is made from the leaves of the hawthorn tree, which has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The main active ingredient in this cream is flavonoids, which are powerful antioxidants that help to relax the muscles in the bladder. When the muscles in the bladder are too tight, it can cause a sudden and strong urge to urinate, leading to overactive bladder symptoms. By relaxing these muscles, the flavonoids in Crataegus monogyna cream may help to reduce the frequency of urination and the urgency to go to the bathroom. Some studies suggest that flavonoids may also help to improve blood flow to the bladder, which can further reduce symptoms of overactive bladder. This cream is often used alongside other treatments, such as medications or lifestyle changes, to help manage symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Equisetum arvense herbal cream that helps with overactive bladder, start by gathering 20-25 grams of dried Equisetum arvense roots and rhizomes. You can purchase these online or from a local herbal store. Next, combine the dried roots and rhizomes in a clean glass jar with 500 milliliters of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil. Allow the mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. After the steeping period, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. Now, add 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1-2 tablespoons of shea butter to the oil mixture and heat it gently in a double boiler until the wax and butter have melted. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool before transferring it to a clean container. Store the Equisetum arvense herbal cream in a cool, dark place and apply it topically to the affected area as needed, ideally 2-3 times a day. Note: Before using the cream, consult with a healthcare professional, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.
remedies.image_alt: Crataegus monogyna herbal creams for overactive bladder

Demethoxycurcumin has been found to help reduce overactive bladder symptoms, likely due to its ability to relax bladder muscles and reduce inflammation, which can help prevent frequent and sudden urges to urinate.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa cream is a natural remedy that can help with overactive bladder. It contains turmeric, a spice with strong anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and irritation in the bladder area. When the bladder is inflamed, it can cause muscle spasms and lead to frequent urination. Turmeric in the cream can help soothe the bladder and calm the muscles, allowing the bladder to function properly. The cream also contains other natural ingredients that can help relax the muscles and improve bladder control. Some people who have tried Curcuma longa cream have reported fewer trips to the bathroom and improved bladder control. The natural ingredients in the cream may also reduce the risk of bladder infections, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms. As a result, Curcuma longa cream can be a good option for people who want to manage their overactive bladder symptoms without medication.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Equisetum arvense herbal cream that helps with overactive bladder, first you need to harvest and dry the Equisetum arvense plant. Equisetum arvense, also known as horsetail, can be found in damp areas and has long, thin stems with small cones. Cut the stems just above the node, then tie them in small bunches and hang them upside down in a warm, dry, dark place. Let the stems dry completely for a few weeks, or until they are brittle and crumbly. Once the Equisetum arvense is dry, crush it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder. Next, mix the powder with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a ratio of about 1 part Equisetum arvense to 5 parts carrier oil. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each heating, until the mixture reaches a temperature of about 150-160°F. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Once the mixture has cooled, strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, discarding the solids. The resulting herbal cream can be stored in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months. Apply a small amount of the cream to the affected area, such as the lower abdomen, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe and calm the bladder.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for overactive bladder

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams, also known as St. John's Wort, may be helpful in treating overactive bladder. This is because it has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the bladder and reduce spasms that can cause urgency and frequency. The cream may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms. The antispasmodic properties can help to calm the muscles and allow them to contract and relax normally, rather than spasming and causing problems. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the bladder, which can also contribute to symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Equisetum arvense herbal cream that helps with overactive bladder, start by gathering fresh or dried Equisetum arvense plant, also known as horsetail. Harvest the plant in the summer months, and clean it thoroughly to remove dirt and debris. If using fresh plant, dry it in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying it for several days. Once the plant is dry, crush it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine the powdered plant with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a small saucepan, and heat it gently over low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture reaches a temperature of around 100°F to 150°F. Remove the saucepan from the heat, and let it cool for a few minutes. Next, add a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax to the mixture, and stir until it's fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar, and let it cool completely before using it. Once the cream has cooled, apply a small amount to the affected area as needed, ideally after urination.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa cream is a natural remedy that can help people with overactive bladder. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Equisetum arvense herbal creams that help with overactive bladder, you will need dried horsetail herb, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. First, infuse the dried horsetail herb in the carrier oil by placing it in a clean glass jar and covering it with the oil. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day, to allow the herbs to release their goodness into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in the double boiler, then slowly add the infused oil to the melted wax, stirring constantly. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and forms a smooth, creamy consistency. Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the cream cool and thicken further. Once it has reached the desired consistency, transfer the Equisetum arvense herbal cream to a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator to slow down the oxidation process. Apply the cream to the affected area as needed, ideally before bedtime, to help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Equisetum arvense herbal cream that helps with overactive bladder, first gather the necessary ingredients and equipment. You'll need dried Equisetum arvense herb, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler or a heat-resistant glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Start by melting the beeswax in the double boiler or heat-resistant bowl, then add the carrier oil and stir until well combined. Next, add the dried Equisetum arvense herb to the mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight to allow the herb's properties to infuse into the oil. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, then discard the solids. Finally, let the mixture cool and thicken before transferring it to a jar or tin for storage. Once the cream is ready, apply a small amount to the affected area as needed to help calm and soothe the bladder, reducing the frequency of urination and alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense, also known as horsetail, is a plant that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including overactive bladder. The cream made from this plant contains silica and other minerals that help to soothe and calm the muscles in the bladder. This can help to reduce the frequency and urgency of urination, and provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with overactive bladder. The silica in the cream also helps to strengthen the muscles in the bladder, making them less prone to spasms and contractions. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Equisetum arvense herbal creams that help with overactive bladder, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Equisetum arvense roots, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a preservative like vitamin E oil. First, combine 2 tablespoons of dried Equisetum arvense roots with 4 cups of boiling water in a heat-proof bowl, and let it steep for 20-30 minutes to make a strong herbal infusion. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, and discard the solids. Next, mix 2 tablespoons of the herbal infusion with 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a small saucepan, and heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax is fully melted. Remove the saucepan from the heat and add 2 tablespoons of carrier oil, stirring well to combine. Allow the mixture to cool and thicken, then add a few drops of vitamin E oil as a preservative. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before using it as a cream to help soothe and calm an overactive bladder.
remedies.image_alt: Equisetum arvense herbal creams for overactive bladder

intro_desc: For people with overactive bladder, using herbal creams can be a helpful remedy. Overactive bladder is a condition where the bladder muscle contracts too often, leading to sudden and strong urges to urinate. If left untreated, it can cause accidents, leakage, and discomfort.  Herbal creams containing Urtica dioica (stinging nettle) and Valeriana officinalis (valerian root) can provide relief. Urtica dioica has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease irritation in the bladder area. This can help to reduce the frequency and urgency of urination. Valeriana officinalis, on the other hand, is a natural relaxant that can help to calm the bladder muscle and reduce spasms.  Fixing overactive bladder is crucial because it can affect daily life in many ways. Leaking or accidents can be embarrassing and stressful, causing people to avoid social events, travel, or even leave their homes. This can lead to feelings of isolation, depression, and anxiety. Improving bladder control can help people regain their confidence, feel more in control, and enjoy their daily activities without worry. By using herbal creams containing Urtica dioica and Valeriana officinalis, people can take a natural step towards managing their symptoms and improving their quality of life.
supplementary_best: For overactive bladder, a combination of Urtica dioica, Avena sativa, and Zingiber officinale creams may be beneficial. Urtica dioica, also known as nettle root, has been traditionally used to support prostate health and reduce urinary issues in men. Avena sativa, or oats, contains saponins and flavonoids that help soothe the bladder and reduce inflammation. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the bladder muscles and improve urine flow. Together, these herbs may help reduce the frequency and urgency of urination, and alleviate symptoms associated with overactive bladder.
supplementary_avoid: If you have an overactive bladder, it's best to avoid herbal creams containing herbs like Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis), Saw Palmetto (Serenoa repens), and Ginkgo biloba. Goldenseal has compounds that can irritate the bladder and make symptoms worse. Saw Palmetto can slow down the bladder's ability to empty, making it harder to urinate. Ginkgo biloba can increase urine production, but it also has a diuretic effect that may worsen overactive bladder symptoms. These herbs can make it more difficult to manage your condition, so it's essential to check the ingredients of any herbal cream before using it.
supplementary_other_remedies: Some natural remedies that can help with overactive bladder include warm baths and pelvic floor exercises. Warm baths can relax the muscles in the bladder and reduce spasms. This is because heat helps to calm down the nerves and muscles, making it easier to control the bladder. Warm baths can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of overactive bladder. Pelvic floor exercises, on the other hand, can strengthen the muscles that support the bladder and help to control the flow of urine. This is because these muscles play a crucial role in holding and releasing urine. When they are weak, the bladder can become overactive. By doing pelvic floor exercises regularly, such as Kegel exercises, you can improve the strength and control of these muscles. This can help to reduce the frequency of urination and the need to urinate urgently.  Another natural remedy that can help with overactive bladder is stress management techniques. Stress and anxiety can trigger the nerves in the bladder and make it more likely to contract and cause symptoms. By practicing stress management techniques, such as deep breathing, meditation, or yoga, you can reduce stress and anxiety and help to control the bladder. This is because these techniques can help to calm down the nerves and reduce muscle tension. Finally, some people find that making lifestyle changes, such as reducing caffeine and alcohol intake, can also help to reduce symptoms of overactive bladder. This is because these substances can irritate the bladder and make it more likely to contract. By making these lifestyle changes, you can reduce the frequency of urination and the need to urinate urgently.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with overactive bladder often experience other health issues. One common problem is urinary tract infections (UTIs). When the bladder muscle contracts too often, it can push bacteria up into the urinary tract, causing infections. The herb uva ursi, also known as bearberry, has been used to treat UTIs due to its antiseptic properties, which help kill the bacteria. Another issue is stress incontinence, where the bladder muscle is too weak to hold urine. The herb saw palmetto has been used to help strengthen the bladder muscle by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.  Some people with overactive bladder also experience constipation, which can put extra pressure on the bladder and make symptoms worse. The herb senna has been used to help stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. Additionally, some people with overactive bladder may experience interstitial cystitis, a chronic condition where the bladder lining becomes inflamed. The herb marshmallow root has been used to help soothe and protect the bladder lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Finally, some people with overactive bladder may experience chronic pelvic pain, which can be caused by inflammation and irritation in the pelvic area. The herb willow bark has been used to help reduce pain and inflammation in the pelvic area due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
